finding: R4 phase-1 kill-metric semantics: measurement book records first-poll costs, diverging 9.8c/share from operated arm

rederived_from_raw.cost_field_semantics: cost = (ask+0.01) + 0.07*p*(1-p); exact for all records carrying f (0 mismatches)
rederived_from_raw.t0_join: 36 measure records vs 27 impulse_v2 ledger trades: 15 sized -> joined (15/15, incl. 1 unsettled), 12 f_nonpos -> joined (entered later), 9 f_nonpos -> no trade. 15+12=27 = every impulse_v2 trade; zero impulse_v2 trades missing from the measure book (no survivorship), zero eng+t0 duplicates in entire 3576-trade ledger (no _src merge dupes), no 600-cap rotation issue (n=27 << cap)

headline_numbers.note_ci: analyst CI [-15.35,+3.33] matches a 1h-block bootstrap (18 blocks); iid CI is wider; both straddle the -2c kill bar

rederived_from_raw.win_field_integrity: win agrees with ledger result on all joined records (0 disagreements) and with Coinbase open(t0) vs open(t0+300) on 35/35 settled; zero exact ties; no lookahead in the finding (win used only for outcome evaluation; f features recorded at sec 0-37 in-window)

merge_agent_flag_resolution.same_signal_check: All 12 rich-first-poll-entered-later records map to a flagship trade with SAME t0, SAME side, entrySec > first-poll sec (12/12), strictly cheaper all-in (12/12, mean 11.19c, range 3.0-38.8c). impulse50 (same signal stream, flat stake) also entered the same t0+side 12/12. Ledger has exactly one impulse_v2 trade per t0 and the bot's risk_ok 'not dup' guard forbids a second entry per (eid,t0). These are re-polls of ONE signal inside the 45s window, not second triggers.
merge_agent_flag_resolution.sized_class_check: All 15 sized records: measure cost == ledger entry all-in to <0.06c and entrySec == f.sec (+/-1s) — sized records really are first-poll fills, so the join classes are clean.
merge_agent_flag_resolution.design_intent: SPLIT. (a) The kill book at first-fillable-poll cost IS pre-registered: FINAL-DESIGN.md SS3.2 pseudocode 'measurement book records ONCE per interval - first fillable poll (matches the CONFIRMED fast-entry regime)' and SS7 Phase 1 kill reads 'measurement-book net/share'. So evaluating the kill on the first-poll counterfactual is design intent -> that half is an AMENDMENT REQUEST, not a bug. (b) The sized/skip FLAGS are a genuine code-vs-design divergence: MF6 pre-registers 'skip is final at t0+45s ... snapshot of last in-window eval', but btc5m_bot.py _measure_record (dedupe line 581) stamps sized/skip from the FIRST fillable poll and increments skips there (line 585), so 12 of 21 f_nonpos records (and the skips counter) misdescribe intervals the flagship actually entered. That half IS a bug versus the pre-registered spec, and it breaks availability/skip accounting.
merge_agent_flag_resolution.design_assumption_violated: The 'matches the CONFIRMED fast-entry regime' rationale is empirically wrong for the sizer: 12/27 (44%) of operated entries happen after a rich first poll, mean 11.2c cheaper. The design did not anticipate the f<=0 wait-then-fill dynamic, so the pre-registered kill input systematically prices the operated policy's worst poll.

kill_evaluability.note: min 200 fills is plausibly reached by day 14 (design assumed ~20/day -> 280), so the concern is timely: at current -6.23c the kill WOULD fire on a book whose operated arm runs +3.55c/share. Fix (store best in-window fillable cost or realized fill alongside first-poll cost) must land before day 14.

corrections_to_claim: Operated flagship is +3.55c/share equal-weighted (+3.49 share-weighted), not +3.57 (immaterial rounding/gas treatment)

The claim's framing 'misclassifies 12 of 27 flagship entries as skips' is correct for the sized flag; the kill-book cost semantics are pre-registered design, so the action item is an amendment plus a flag-semantics bugfix, not purely a bug report
n=35 at 14.6/day reaches ~205 by day 14, so 'kill not yet evaluable' will stop being true right around the kill date - strengthens urgency
